query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in California, within PG&E's territory, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
Based on the OpenEI tariff data for PG&E (EIA ID 14328), here are the **active** commercial demand charges for customers with demand >500 kW:

---

## PG&E Commercial Demand Charges (Demand >500 kW)

All active tariffs returned are under the **E-19 Medium General Demand** family (PG&E's rate class for customers ≥500 kW). Below are four distinct service classes with their demand charges:

### 1. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU (Secondary)
| Demand Component | Rate |
|---|---|
| **Flat Demand (Summer & Winter)** | **$42.10/kW** |
| Max-Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Part-Peak TOU Demand | $16.44/kW |
| Off-Peak TOU Demand | $21.59/kW |
| Fixed Charge | $57.12/day |

### 2. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU Option R (Primary)
| Demand Component | Rate |
|---|---|
| **Flat Demand (Summer & Winter)** | **$33.10/kW** |
| Max-Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Part-Peak TOU Demand | $14.86/kW |
| Off-Peak TOU Demand | $18.71/kW |
| Fixed Charge | $84.72/day |

### 3. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU Option R (Secondary)
| Demand Component | Rate |
|---|---|
| **Flat Demand (Summer & Winter)** | **$42.10/kW** |
| Max-Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Peak TOU Demand | $0.00/kW |
| Part-Peak TOU Demand | $16.44/kW |
| Off-Peak TOU Demand | $21.59/kW |
| Fixed Charge | $51.12/day |

### 4. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU Option R (Transmission)
| Demand Component | Rate |
|---|---|
| **Flat Demand** | **$16.47/kW** |
| TOU Demand (all periods) | $0.00/kW |
| Fixed Charge | $110.17/day |

---

### Key Takeaways

- **Flat demand charges** are the primary demand component and range from **$16.47/kW (Transmission)** to **$42.10/kW (Secondary)** depending on service voltage level.
- The **Option R** variants ("R" = Rate) are slightly different tariff sub-types; the non-R Secondary TOU has a higher fixed charge ($57.12/day vs. $51.12/day) but identical demand rates.
- There are also **Voluntary** versions of each (with much lower fixed charges ~$11.37/day) for customers who opt in — these carry the same demand rates as their mandatory counterparts.
- The rates shown take effect **March 2026** (start date: 1774594800 Unix timestamp) per OpenEI.

> **Source:** OpenEI IURDB, Pacific Gas & Electric Co (EIA ID 14328), retrieved via `get_utility_tariffs` for Commercial sector in San Jose, CA and Oakland, CA (PG&E service territory).
